parsed_review.summary: CP00-272 is a clean, descriptor-only foundation pack that bootstraps the RP08 (Email And Office Native DMS) program. The pack-level production-ready posture is supported and honestly stated: the manifest and command-evidence record production_ready=false with status "implemented_pending_review"/"implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout", while the Hermes evidence packet reports only production_ready_candidate=true gated on coverage.valid && foundation.valid. Coverage reconciles exactly across every artifact: 150 RP08 units (RP08.P00.M00.S01 -> RP08.P01.M02.S08) in the live closeout-pack-plan, review-baseline, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, registry pack binding, and the serialized contract; deliverable distribution sums to 150 (implementation 108, contract 8, security_audit 16, hermes_evidence 7, claude_review 3, ui 8) and the micro-phase/micro-title distributions match the validator's expectations. The cursor chain is consistent (CP00-271 next_subphase RP08.P00.M00.S01 -> CP00-272 -> CP00-273 first RP08.P01.M02.S09). Implementation (registry.js/service.js/validators.js/index.js) is fully Object.freeze descriptor data with explicit no-write attestations; contracts/email-dms-core-contract.json is a faithful projection that the validator/test deep-equal against the in-code objects, and package.json wires both the rp08:email-dms-core:validate script and the test glob. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold throughout: no email/Office-native/sync/filing runtime, no permission evaluation, no audit/state/object-storage writes, no command runtime, and no Hermes runtime receipt (Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic descriptor review-packet/receipt shapes, not product runtime). The authority boundary is preserved everywhere: claude_is_final_approval=false, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, and human approval required before any runtime opening. CP178+ review-hardening is satisfied: the auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (exit 0), the prompt excludes full diff/source with read_tools_used inspection (summary-only prohibited and not used), the schema is present, and tools are restricted to Read/Grep/Glob. The referenced raw-output.json and review-receipt.json are absent because they are produced by THIS read_only_claude_run step of the hardened sequence (correct, not a defect), and the untracked c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json belongs to a separate LDIP review chain and is correctly out of scope. One P3 cosmetic finding: changed-file-scope.json redundantly lists active pack directories under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved; the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs and the manifest worktree policy are correct, so there is no scope leakage. Note for transparency: at 150 units the pack sits exactly at the risk-class-C ceiling (40-150), which is within policy. No P0/P1/P2 issues; the pack is ready for post-review closeout subject to the normal human/final-approval gate.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists active pack directories under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: The unrelated_dirty_files_preserved array (lines 84-90) includes packages/email-dms/ and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-272/, which are core to CP00-272 and are also (correctly) enumerated as active pack files via active_pack_implementation_refs (lines 5-16) and untracked_implementation_files (lines 76-82). The duplication stems from git reporting untracked content at directory granularity (git status shows '?? packages/email-dms/' rather than individual files), and the scope generator copying every untracked git row into unrelated_dirty_files_preserved while separately resolving the active implementation files. There is no actual scope leakage: active_pack_implementation_refs lists the precise pack files, and the authoritative manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 6283-6286) correctly lists ONLY the unrelated .docx and the separate-chain docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json. So this is a presentational/tooling inconsistency, not a substantive misclassification.
findings.recommendation: Have the changed-file-scope generator exclude any path already resolved into active_pack_implementation_refs from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so the artifact matches the manifest's worktree_transition_policy. Non-blocking; no pack content change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational nit. No change required for CP00-272 closeout; active_pack_implementation_refs and the manifest worktree policy are authoritative and correct. Recommend tightening the scope generator in a future review-tooling pass.
